operating_state: Revenue reached $7.10 billion in Q3 FY2022, up 50% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for NVIDIA Ampere architecture products across all platforms. Gaming revenue increased 42% on higher GeForce GPU sales, while Data Center revenue surged 55% from hyperscale customer adoption for cloud computing and AI workloads. Professional Visualization revenue jumped 144% as enterprises deployed hybrid work solutions, though Automotive revenue grew only 8% due to supply constraints.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 73.0% compared to 65.1% in the prior year period, benefiting from higher-end product mix within desktop and notebook GeForce GPUs, improved manufacturing yields, and reduced acquisition-related costs. Operating expenses increased 25% year-over-year driven by employee growth, higher compensation including stock-based compensation, and increased infrastructure costs. The company maintained supply-constrained operations with prepayments for long-term supply agreements and non-cancellable inventory orders.
material_events: NVIDIA paid $100 million in quarterly cash dividends and repaid $1.00 billion of 2.20% Notes Due 2021 in August. The company announced general availability of NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise and 65 new software development kits at GTC conference. Nearly all desktop Ampere architecture GeForce GPU shipments incorporated Lite Hash Rate technology to direct products toward gamers rather than cryptocurrency miners.
macro_exposures: Supply chain constraints from limited foundry capacity and component availability continue to impact production scaling and may result in higher input costs. Cryptocurrency market volatility, including the pending Ethereum 2.0 standard transition, may reduce GPU mining demand and increase aftermarket resales affecting retail pricing and channel inventory. COVID-19 pandemic effects continue creating demand forecasting difficulties and potential supply chain disruptions.
forward_outlook: Management expects to remain supply-constrained into fiscal year 2023 and continues placing advance inventory orders with premiums and deposits to secure capacity. The company maintains $7.24 billion in share repurchase authorization through December 2022 and continues quarterly dividend payments subject to board determination. NVIDIA announced plans to build Earth-2 AI supercomputer for climate change prediction and expanded partnerships with VMware for AI-ready enterprise platforms.
uncertainty_profile: The Arm acquisition faces potential termination by September 2022 if regulatory approvals are not obtained, with $1.25 billion of advanced consideration at risk following FTC concerns and Phase 2 reviews in the UK and EU. The company holds $638 million in unrecognized tax benefits with uncertain timing for resolution and faces IRS examination for fiscal years 2018-2019. Long manufacturing lead times create significant supply-demand mismatch risks, potentially resulting in product shortages or excess inventory charges if demand patterns shift.
